sections.title: Schools
sections.intro: Georgetown ISD. Ford Elementary is walking distance. Benold Middle is a short drive.

cards.title: Jo Ann Ford Elementary
cards.brief: Top 10% Texas elementary, walking distance from home. PK–5, 538 students.
cards.desc: Ranked 461st of 4,651 Texas elementary schools. Student-teacher ratio 15:1. Strong Gifted & Talented program (top 15% statewide). Same street as our house — Younger Son can walk or bike.

cards.title: Douglas Benold Middle School
cards.brief: Assigned middle school for Older Son. Grades 6–8, ~8 min drive.

meta.text: 1401 N Parmer Ranch Blvd, Georgetown, TX 78633

cards.desc: School clubs and sports happen here — soccer, band, theater, robotics. Join clubs early; that's the fastest way for Older Son to make friends at a new school.

cards.title: Georgetown High School
cards.brief: Future high school for both boys. ~12 min drive. Strong athletics and band.
cards.desc: Assigned high school for both boys eventually. 12,500-seat stadium. Strong football and band programs typical of Texas high schools.

sections.title: After-School Care
sections.intro: Options for both boys after school ends at ~3 PM. YMCA at Ford Elementary is the standout for Younger Son.

cards.title: YMCA Y Afterschool at Ford Elementary
cards.brief: On-site at Ford Elementary. No pickup needed until 6:30 PM. K–5.
cards.desc: Runs inside the school building — no transportation needed. Homework help, physical activity, STEAM enrichment, snack. Register early — popular slots fill before the school year starts.

cards.title: Boys & Girls Club of Georgetown
cards.brief: Backup option, ~12 min. Mentoring, tutoring, sports. Both boys eligible.

meta.text: Hours: Mon–Fri 3–6:30 PM; early-out from 1 PM

cards.desc: Backup option if YMCA fills up. Good for Older Son who's past elementary YMCA. Mentoring, tutoring, sports, summer camp. Transportation from some schools may be available.

cards.title: Middle School Clubs & Sports (Benold)
cards.brief: Older Son's practical after-school path: school clubs, theater, soccer, band.
cards.desc: For Older Son, the social on-ramp at a new school is participating in clubs: theater, robotics, band, art, chess, and athletics: track, cross-country, soccer. Sign up at the start of each semester.

sections.title: Sports & Activities
sections.intro: Ninja Warrior (both love it from Frisco) and soccer. Local options to keep the boys active.

cards.title: Ninja Nation Austin
cards.brief: Same brand from Frisco. ~30 min drive. Classes, open gym, camps.

meta.text: Format: Weekly classes, open gym, camps

cards.desc: Same brand the boys already love — programs and obstacles will feel familiar. World-class course, coached classes by age/level, open gym, and summer camps. Book a trial class soon after the move.

cards.title: Move Sport Ninja Academy
cards.brief: Former ANW competitor's gym. Pflugerville, ~30 min. Competition-track training.

meta.text: Coach: Andy Lowes (former American Ninja Warrior)

cards.desc: Smaller, more personal than Ninja Nation. Run by a former ANW competitor with 15+ years of gymnastics coaching. Strong fit if the boys want competition-track training.

cards.title: Georgetown Soccer Association (GSA)
cards.brief: Est. 1981. Rec + Select leagues, ~10 min. Fall & spring seasons.

meta.text: Ray Thomas Jr. Fields, 2101 Maple St, Georgetown

cards.desc: 450+ recreational players plus Select/Academy programs (Force FC). US Soccer curriculum, volunteer parent coaches, age-grouped teams. Recreational league is the right starting point. Register 1–2 months before season start.

cards.title: City of Georgetown Youth Soccer League
cards.brief: Cheaper city-run rec league. $60 resident. San Gabriel Park, Saturday games.

meta.text: San Gabriel Park, 425 E Morrow St

cards.desc: Lower-pressure alternative to GSA. City-run, Saturday games. Good first soccer experience or complement.

cards.title: AcroTex Gymnastics — Ninja Classes
cards.brief: Combined gymnastics + ninja in Round Rock. ~25 min. Closer alternative.
cards.desc: Combined gymnastics + ninja classes. Backup option closer to home if Austin commute gets old.

cards.title: Sport Clips Family YMCA — Georgetown
cards.brief: Family membership covers basketball, swim, soccer, gym. ~12 min, ~$80–100/mo.
cards.desc: Basketball, soccer, swim lessons, summer day camps for kids — plus gym and fitness classes for parents. Single family membership covers many programs. Strong value.

sections.title: Theater, Acting & Music Lessons
sections.intro: Older Son loves acting and singing. Younger Son wants to play drums. Programs near Georgetown.

cards.title: Georgetown Palace Theatre — Education Dept.
cards.brief: Year-round acting, musical theater, voice, improv. Grades 1–12. Downtown, ~10 min.

meta.text: Programs: Acting, musical theater, voice, improv, dance, film & TV, stage combat
Format: After-school classes, weekend classes, seasonal camps

cards.desc: The hub for performing arts in Georgetown. Year-round classes for grades 1–12. After-school and weekend options, plus summer/break camps with early drop-off and late pick-up. Perfect for Older Son's acting and singing interests. Historic 1926 theater on the Square.

cards.title: Arts Avenue — Georgetown
cards.brief: Theater + dance classes downtown. Multiple skill levels.

meta.text: 610 N Austin Ave, Suite 125, Georgetown, TX 78628

cards.desc: Theater classes alongside dance programs. Multiple skill levels. Alternative to the Palace Theatre for Older Son's acting interest.

cards.title: kidsActing Studio
cards.brief: 16 Austin-area locations (including Round Rock, Cedar Park, Pflugerville). Pre-K through high school.

meta.text: 16 Austin metro locations (incl. Round Rock & Cedar Park)

cards.desc: Long-running theater program focusing on self-esteem, acting, and musical theater for Pre-K through high school. While their main studio is in Austin, they hold camps and classes at 16 local satellite locations. Convenient nearby options include Cedar Park (Live Oak Unitarian Church, Cedar Park Dance Co.), Round Rock (Grace Presbyterian, The Dance Spot, The Dance Gallery), and Pflugerville (Dance Elements) — all within a 15–20 minute drive from our house.

cards.title: Austin Drum Lab — Georgetown
cards.brief: Kid-friendly drum studio in Georgetown. Decades of touring/teaching experience.
cards.desc: Run by Tony Miller who has decades of touring and teaching experience. Known for kid-friendly, patient teaching style. Right here in Georgetown — no Austin commute needed for drum lessons.

cards.title: Clark's Music Class — Georgetown
cards.brief: Private drums, guitar, piano. Patient, positive approach. Georgetown-based.
cards.desc: Private instruction with a patient, positive approach. Also offers guitar and piano if Younger Son wants to try other instruments later. Serves Georgetown, Round Rock, Hutto area.

cards.title: Bach to Rock — Cedar Park
cards.brief: Private lessons + band rehearsals. Kids perform live. ~25 min.
cards.desc: Private drum lessons plus ensemble programs (Band/Glee Club). Kids get to perform live — great for building confidence. Fun, accessible approach for various ages and skill levels.

cards.title: School of Rock — Round Rock
cards.brief: Private lessons + weekly band rehearsals → live performances. ~20 min.
cards.desc: Combines private lessons with weekly band rehearsals. Students prepare to perform live in front of audiences. Could be great for both Younger Son (drums) and Older Son (singing) to jam together.

sections.title: Gaming, Esports & Spooky Fun
sections.intro: Both boys love Fortnite. Older Son is into Five Nights at Freddy's and horror. Places to play and compete.

cards.title: The Esports Cave — Cedar Park
cards.brief: Dedicated gaming center. Tournaments, birthday parties, high-end PCs. ~25 min.

meta.text: Format: Open play, tournaments, birthday parties

cards.desc: Dedicated facility for gaming with high-end PCs and consoles. Hosts tournaments (including Fortnite), birthday parties, and drop-in sessions. Both boys can play on proper setups with other kids. Great birthday party venue too.

cards.title: XP League — Youth Esports
cards.brief: Structured youth esports league. Ages 7–17. Coached teams, regional tournaments.

meta.text: Games: Fortnite, Rocket League, Super Smash Bros

cards.desc: Professional youth esports experience: structured teams, coaching, regional and national tournaments. Like organized sports but for gaming. Good if the boys want to compete seriously in Fortnite or Rocket League.

cards.title: YMCA eSports Programs
cards.brief: YMCA offers Gamers Night Out, eSports Academy. Strategy & sportsmanship focus.

meta.text: CHASCO Family YMCA / Twin Lakes YMCA
Programs: Intro classes, Gamers Night Out, eSports Academy

cards.desc: The YMCA offers structured eSports programs focused on strategy, communication, and sportsmanship. Lower-key than XP League. Check if included with our family membership.

cards.title: House of Torment — Austin
cards.brief: Austin's biggest haunted house. Professional sets & animatronics. Halloween season.
cards.desc: One of Austin's most well-known haunted attractions. Professional sets, animatronics, live actors. High intensity — perfect for Older Son's FNAF/Little Nightmares vibe. Check age recommendations before taking Younger Son.

cards.title: Scream Hollow — Smithville
cards.brief: Wicked Halloween Park — haunted hayrides, multiple attractions. ~1 hr drive.
cards.desc: Extensive Wicked Halloween Park experience with haunted hayrides and multiple themed attractions. Outdoor setting. Combine with a Hill Country drive. Great for Older Son's horror interest.

cards.title: Escape Rooms — Austin / Round Rock
cards.brief: Horror-themed & family-friendly escape rooms. Multiple venues in the area.

meta.text: Options: Escapology (Round Rock, ~20 min), The Escape Game (Austin, ~40 min), 4 Dreams Escape (Austin)

cards.desc: Several escape rooms nearby: Escapology in Round Rock has family-friendly private rooms, The Escape Game in Austin has kid-friendly options, and 4 Dreams has creepier themes (zombie labs, abandoned attics) that Older Son would love. Great for birthday parties.

sections.title: Gardening & Plant Shops
sections.intro: Older Son loves gardening. Local nurseries and plant shops to buy flowers, learn about native plants, and get supplies.

cards.title: McIntire's Garden Center
cards.brief: Local staple since 1976. Native plants, trees, expert advice. Leander Rd.
cards.desc: Georgetown institution since 1976. Wide selection of native and hardy plants, trees, and professional gardening supplies. Knowledgeable staff can help Older Son learn about Texas gardening — different climate from what we're used to. Great for native wildflowers.

cards.title: Hillside Nursery — Williams Dr
cards.brief: Full-service garden center on Williams Dr. Plants, flowers, pottery. Closest nursery.
cards.desc: Right on Williams Drive — probably our closest nursery. Wide variety of plants, flowers, trees, and pottery. Knowledgeable staff. Perfect for quick weekend trips with Older Son to grab flowers.

cards.title: Root to Rise Plant Co.
cards.brief: Boutique houseplant shop near Georgetown Square. Indoor plants, pots, repotting.
cards.desc: Cute boutique specializing in houseplants, pots, and plant accessories. They offer repotting services too. Fun for Older Son to browse for indoor plants for his room. Near the Square so combine with a downtown walk.

sections.title: Georgetown — Parks & Daily Life
sections.intro: Everything within 15 minutes of home. More nature, water, and free family activities than you'd expect.

cards.title: Lake Georgetown & Trails
cards.brief: 1,300-acre reservoir. Goodwater Loop Trail, swimming, kayaking. ~10 min.

meta.text: Cost: Free entry; $5 parking some days

cards.desc: 1,300-acre reservoir surrounded by the Goodwater Loop Trail (26.6 miles total). Cedar Breaks Park has a boat ramp and swimming beach. Russell Park has campsites. Great Saturday outing — fishing, kayaking, hiking, picnicking.

cards.title: San Gabriel Park
cards.brief: 180-acre central park. Playgrounds, splash pad, river, BBQ. Free.
cards.desc: 180-acre central park with playgrounds, BBQ pits, splash pad, river access, and trails along the San Gabriel River. Hosts most city festivals and free events. Best free family destination in town.

cards.title: Garey Park
cards.brief: 525-acre nature park. Splash pad, dog park, river. $5 vehicle entry.
cards.desc: 525-acre nature park. Splash pad, dog park, equestrian trails, river access. Hosts Family Nature Fest in April. Often less crowded than San Gabriel Park.

cards.title: Blue Hole Park
cards.brief: Natural swimming spot downtown. Limestone bluffs. Free. Get there early.
cards.desc: Natural swimming spot on the South San Gabriel River in downtown. Beloved by locals on hot summer days. Limestone bluffs, shaded picnic area. Get there early on weekends — fills up fast.

cards.title: Inner Space Cavern
cards.brief: Underground cave tours. Walking + Wild Cave adventure. ~$15–25.
cards.desc: Underground cave system right off I-35. Multiple tour levels including "Wild Cave" adventure tours where kids can crawl through real passages. Memorable rainy-day option.

cards.title: Georgetown Public Library
cards.brief: Free kids programs, STEM workshops, summer reading. Library cards for everyone.
cards.desc: Modern library with free kids programs — Paws for People (kids read to therapy dogs), STEM workshops, summer reading program. Get library cards for everyone in the first month.

cards.title: Downtown Georgetown Square
cards.brief: One of Texas's prettiest squares. Free events most weekends. ~10 min.
cards.desc: One of the prettiest downtown squares in Texas. Free events almost every weekend: Pistons on the Square (3rd Saturday, classic cars), Second Saturday Market Days (Mar–Nov), Red Poppy Festival (late April), Christmas Stroll. Coffee shops, candy stores, indie boutiques.

sections.title: Day Trips (Under 1 Hour)
sections.intro: Half-day or full-day outings without overnight planning. Great Saturday options.

cards.title: Round Rock — Outlets & Old Settlers Park
cards.brief: Outlet mall + 645-acre park. ~20 min. Shopping + outdoor combo.
cards.desc: Round Rock has the outlet mall (great for kids' clothes/shoes) and Old Settlers Park (645 acres, baseball complex, fishing pond, playgrounds, disc golf).

cards.title: Cedar Park / Brushy Creek Lake Park
cards.brief: H-E-B Center (concerts, hockey), Lakeline Mall, splash pad. ~30 min.
cards.desc: Cedar Park has H-E-B Center (concerts, Texas Stars hockey, Austin Spurs basketball), Lakeline Mall, and the gourmet H-E-B. Brushy Creek Lake Park has fishing, paddle boards, and a great splash pad.

cards.title: Typhoon Texas — Pflugerville
cards.brief: Large water park. Slides, wave pool, lazy river. ~25 min. ~$45/person.
cards.desc: Closest large water park — slides, wave pool, lazy river. Buy season passes if going more than 2× per summer.

cards.title: Downtown Austin
cards.brief: Capitol, Lady Bird Lake, Zilker, Barton Springs, bats at sunset. ~40 min.
cards.desc: Texas State Capitol (free tours), Lady Bird Lake hike-and-bike trail, Zilker Park, Barton Springs swimming pool, the bats under Congress Bridge at sunset (March–October).

cards.title: Thinkery (Austin Children's Museum)
cards.brief: Hands-on STEM museum. Maker spaces, water tables. Free Wed 4–8 PM. ~40 min.
cards.desc: Hands-on STEM museum aimed at K–5. Maker spaces, water tables, light tables, outdoor garden. Excellent rainy-day Austin destination.

cards.title: McKinney Falls State Park
cards.brief: Two waterfalls, swimming holes, hiking. $6 adult, kids free. ~55 min.
cards.desc: Two waterfalls, swimming holes, hiking, biking, camping. Less crowded than Barton Springs. Best in spring and fall.

sections.title: Weekend Adventures (1–3 Hours)
sections.intro: Saturday-morning-departure trips. Hill Country starts ~1 hour west; Gulf Coast ~3.5 hours south.

cards.title: Fredericksburg & Hill Country
cards.brief: German town, peach orchards, Enchanted Rock, wineries. ~1h 30min west.
cards.desc: German heritage town, peach orchards (summer), wineries, Enchanted Rock State Park (huge granite dome — great climbing/hiking for kids), Luckenbach. Best March–May (wildflowers) and Oct–Nov (cooler).

cards.title: San Antonio — Riverwalk, SeaWorld, Six Flags
cards.brief: The Alamo, Riverwalk, SeaWorld, Six Flags, SA Zoo. ~1h 30min south.
cards.desc: The Alamo and Riverwalk are bucket-list. SeaWorld San Antonio and Six Flags Fiesta Texas are both there. San Antonio Zoo is one of the best in Texas. Easy overnight weekend.

cards.title: Waco — Magnolia, Zoo, Dr Pepper Museum
cards.brief: Magnolia Market, Cameron Park Zoo, Baylor campus. ~1h 30min north.
cards.desc: Magnolia Market at the Silos (Chip & Joanna Gaines), Dr Pepper Museum, Cameron Park Zoo, Baylor campus. Long day trip or overnight.

cards.title: Schlitterbahn New Braunfels
cards.brief: One of the world's largest water parks. Comal River runs through it. ~1h 15min.
cards.desc: One of the largest water parks in the world. The Comal River runs through it. Plan for a full day; arrive at opening. Season May–September.

cards.title: Gruene — Float the Guadalupe & Dance Hall
cards.brief: River tubing + oldest dance hall in Texas (1878). ~1h 15min south.
cards.desc: Tubing/floating on the Guadalupe or Comal River — peak Texas summer experience. Gruene Hall is the oldest continuously operating dance hall in Texas (1878). Easy combo with a Schlitterbahn weekend.

cards.title: Galveston & Gulf Coast
cards.brief: Closest beach. Pleasure Pier, Moody Gardens. ~3h 30min. Better as 2–3 night trip.
cards.desc: The closest beach. Pleasure Pier, Moody Gardens (pyramid aquarium + rainforest), Schlitterbahn Galveston. Better as a 2–3 night trip than a day trip.

cards.title: Dallas / Fort Worth
cards.brief: Previous home area. Stockyards, Perot Museum, DFW Airport. ~3 hours north.
cards.desc: Previous home and friends/family. DFW Airport for international travel. Fort Worth Stockyards (cattle drives, rodeo), Perot Museum, Klyde Warren Park.

cards.title: Houston — Space Center, Zoo, Museums
cards.brief: NASA Space Center, Houston Zoo, museums. ~2h 30min east. Plan 2-day weekend.
cards.desc: Space Center Houston (NASA), Houston Zoo, Museum of Natural Science, Children's Museum of Houston. Plan for a 2-day weekend to make it worth the drive.

sections.title: Restaurants
sections.intro: Texas food culture is real. BBQ, Tex-Mex, breakfast tacos, and more. Expand as we explore.

cards.title: Monument Cafe
cards.brief: Local institution. Breakfast all day, garden-fresh, famous chocolate icebox pie. ~10 min.

meta.text: Vibe: Classic diner with farm-to-table sensibility

cards.desc: Local institution. Breakfast all day, garden-fresh sides, famous chocolate icebox pie. Good for Saturday morning family breakfast.

cards.title: Wildfire Restaurant
cards.brief: Wood-grilled steaks & seafood on the Square. Upscale but kid-friendly. ~10 min.
cards.desc: Wood-grilled steaks and seafood. On the Square. Upscale but kid-friendly. Good Sunday brunch.

cards.title: Encantada Cocina + Cantina (Formerly El Monumento)
cards.brief: Upscale Tex-Mex & cocktails. River patio, beautiful modern interior. ~10 min.
cards.desc: Newly opened in place of the beloved El Monumento. Reimagined with upscale modern Tex-Mex, signature hand-shaken margaritas, and a gorgeous patio overlooking the San Gabriel River. Close to the Square.

cards.title: 600 Degrees Pizzeria
cards.brief: Wood-fired Neapolitan pizza downtown. Quick, kid-friendly, parents can have beer. ~10 min.
cards.desc: Wood-fired Neapolitan pizza, downtown Square. Quick, good for the kids, parents can have a beer.

cards.title: Tony & Luigi's
cards.brief: Italian-American. House-made pasta, big portions, kids' menu. ~10 min.
cards.desc: Italian-American. House-made pasta, big portions, kids' menu. Reliable family dinner spot.

cards.title: Franklin Barbecue
cards.brief: Most famous BBQ in America. Line up by 9 AM or pre-order online. ~40 min.
cards.desc: The most famous BBQ in America. Brisket is the legend. Worth doing once. Skip the wait via online pre-order.

cards.title: Terry Black's BBQ
cards.brief: Excellent BBQ, much shorter line than Franklin. Family-friendly. ~40 min.
cards.desc: Excellent BBQ, much shorter line than Franklin. Family-friendly. The practical "best BBQ" choice for a regular dinner.

cards.title: Veracruz All Natural
cards.brief: Best breakfast tacos in Austin. Migas taco is essential. Multiple locations.
cards.desc: Best breakfast tacos in Austin (according to many). Migas taco is essential. Trailer-turned-empire.

cards.title: Uchi / Uchiko
cards.brief: Top-tier omakase / modern Japanese. Reservations needed. Special occasion.
cards.desc: Top-tier omakase / modern Japanese. Reservations needed. Special occasion.

cards.title: Round Rock Donuts
cards.brief: Since 1926. Famous Texas-size yellow donuts. Saturday morning kid bribery. ~20 min.
cards.desc: Local institution since 1926. Famous "Texas-size" yellow donuts. Saturday morning kid bribery, sorted.

cards.title: Korean Grocery & BBQ — H Mart & North Austin
cards.brief: H Mart (Lakeline) grocery + food court, Charm K-BBQ. Massive selection. ~20–25 min.

meta.text: Drive: ~20–25 min (N Austin, Lakeline / Howard Ln)

cards.desc: H Mart Lakeline is a massive, modern Korean supermarket featuring a incredible food court (Korean BBQ, fried chicken, bakery, noodles). For all-you-can-eat K-BBQ, Charm Korean BBQ (off Howard Lane) is a popular option. Excellent monthly run for groceries and Korean comfort food.

sections.title: Events, Festivals & Live Music
sections.intro: Austin is the live music capital of the world. Georgetown has its own great festival calendar.

cards.title: Red Poppy Festival (Georgetown)
cards.brief: Georgetown's signature 3-day festival. Parade, live music, Kids' Fun Zone. Free. Late April.
cards.desc: Georgetown's signature festival. Parade, live music, Kids' Fun Zone, POPP Pup Parade, food trucks. On and around the Square. Don't miss it.

cards.title: ACL — Austin City Limits
cards.brief: Major music festival. Two weekends in October, Zilker Park. Kids under 10 free.
cards.desc: Major music festival. Kids under 10 free with paying adult. Family stage area. Tickets sell out fast — buy by April.

cards.title: SXSW (Austin)
cards.brief: Massive film/music/tech fest. Mid-March. Many free showcases, but brutal traffic.
cards.desc: Massive film/music/tech festival. Most events need badges, but many free showcases. Austin traffic is brutal during these 10 days — avoid downtown unless attending.

cards.title: Texas Renaissance Festival
cards.brief: Largest Ren Faire in America. Costumes, jousting, dragons. Oct–Nov. ~3 hours.
cards.desc: Largest Ren Faire in America. Costumes, jousting, food, dragons. Both boys will love it. Best as an overnight trip.

cards.title: Christmas Stroll (Georgetown)
cards.brief: Tree lighting, carriage rides, hot cocoa. First weekend in December.
cards.desc: Tree lighting on the Square, carriage rides, hot cocoa, vendor booths. The annual Georgetown holiday tradition.

cards.title: Two Step Inn — Country Music Festival
cards.brief: Major country fest at San Gabriel Park, Georgetown. Top headliners. April.
cards.desc: Major country music festival hosted right in town. Top-tier headliners.

cards.title: H-E-B Center at Cedar Park
cards.brief: ~8K-seat arena. Touring acts, Texas Stars hockey. ~25 min — closer than Austin.
cards.desc: Mid-size arena (~8,000 seats). Major touring acts, Texas Stars hockey, Austin Spurs basketball. Closer than driving to Austin's arenas.

cards.title: Moody Center (Austin)
cards.brief: New arena on UT campus. Biggest touring acts now play here. ~40 min.
cards.desc: Brand new arena on UT campus. Biggest touring acts play here.

cards.title: Gruene Hall
cards.brief: Oldest dance hall in Texas (1878). Live music, many free shows. ~1h 15min.
cards.desc: The oldest continuously operating dance hall in Texas (1878). Pair with tubing the Guadalupe. Many free shows and legends-pass-through nights.

sections.title: Community & Online Resources
sections.intro: Where neighbors talk. Join these in the first week. Local Facebook groups beat any news site.

cards.title: Nextdoor
cards.brief: Neighborhood app. Lost pets, contractor recs, local sales. Download day one.
cards.desc: The most useful app for the Woodlake/Williams Dr neighborhood. Lost pets, contractor recommendations, package theft alerts, local sales. Download immediately after move-in.

cards.title: JoAnn Ford Elementary — Facebook
cards.brief: Official school page. Announcements, events, PTA. Follow before school starts.
cards.desc: School announcements, event photos, PTA notices. Follow before school year starts.

cards.title: Cool Moms of Georgetown
cards.brief: Active Facebook group. Playdates, restaurant tips, contractor referrals.
cards.desc: Active local moms group. Playdates, classroom volunteer asks, restaurant tips, contractor referrals. Worth joining for wife.

cards.title: All Things Kids In Georgetown
cards.brief: 6K+ followers. Curated family activities, events, classes. Best kids event feed.
cards.desc: Curated kids events, classes, camps, family activities. The single best feed for "what's happening this weekend for the boys."

cards.title: Do512 Family
cards.brief: Best weekly roundup of family events in Greater Austin. Subscribe to newsletter.
cards.desc: Best weekly roundup of family-friendly events in Greater Austin. Newsletter is worth subscribing to.

cards.title: Hello Georgetown
cards.brief: Local news/event blog. Weekly digest of Georgetown happenings.
cards.desc: Curated weekly digest of Georgetown happenings, restaurant openings, school news, civic issues. More useful than the local paper.

cards.title: City of Georgetown — Parks & Rec
cards.brief: All city-run programs: youth sports, swim, library events. Bookmark.
cards.desc: All city-run programs: youth sports, summer camps, swim lessons, library events.

sections.title: Home Services & Utilities
sections.intro: Infrastructure of daily life. Add referrals as we find good providers.

cards.title: Georgetown Utility Systems (GUS)
cards.brief: Municipal electricity. No provider choice. Set up before move-in.
cards.desc: City-owned utility. No retail electricity shopping (unlike Frisco/Oncor). Set up account before move-in.

cards.title: Internet Options
cards.brief: Spectrum cable, AT&T Fiber (check our address), Tachus fiber expanding.
cards.desc: Check fiber availability at our specific address — best speeds and lowest latency for ML/remote work. Spectrum is the cable backup.

cards.title: Grocery — H-E-B
cards.brief: 3651 Williams Dr. ~5 min. Texans love H-E-B. Curbside pickup via app.
cards.desc: Texans love H-E-B. Excellent house brand. Curbside pickup via the H-E-B app saves hours weekly.

cards.title: Costco — Cedar Park
cards.brief: Closest Costco. Bulk buys, kids' clothes, electronics. ~25 min.
cards.desc: Closest Costco. Bulk buys, kids' clothes, electronics. Worth the membership.
